notes: SHIP v1.14.262 - VOID SCENE (photoreal, Ship 2 of the INSPECT-3D MIRROR port; owner chose option A). Strips the sci-fi diorama dressing from the reh3d Inspection Bay so the rack reads as a clean product shot in near-black: hides motes/particles, background ghost-fleet wireframe racks, backdrop gradient panel, floor glow ring, scan-wave + halo, the inspection PAD group (glass slab/edge strips/calibration marks/sweep), practical lights and the slow sweep light. KEEPS the dark reflective floor + the mirror reflection. Mount bg -> true black #000000. Implementation: one VOID_SCENE flag + a prune loop after scene.add(bayGrp) sets visible=false on every bayGrp child except floorMesh and refl - so teardown scene.traverse still disposes them (no GL leak) and bayTick animations no-op on hidden objects. DEVIATION (reported, CLAUDE.md role rule): CLEAR-PORT S3 says DELETE the floor slab and let the reflection ground the void - but BAY_REFLECT is DPR<=2.1, so the reflection is gated OFF on the iPhone (DPR 3); a floorless void would float the rack in black on Johns device. Kept the reflective floor as device-safe grounding; John can rule pure-void-no-floor on device (accepts reflection-off float on iPhone). Mirror/piano-black material finish is the NEXT ship (S2 of CLEAR-PORT), not this one. All inside rackElevation_render3D (reh3d bay), body.rd-only -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical; forge3d untouched; .260 rails/ticks + ghost reflection preserved. Gates: node --check 4 blocks; CSS braces balanced; CRLF preserved; NUL 0; three-stamp .261->.262. NOT device-verified - John iPhone gate pending.
